stemHtml: Explain how the ground heats the lower levels of the atmosphere...

options.a: Lower layers of air are heated by conduction, which means transferring heat by molecular interactions between ground and air.
options.b: Lower layers of air are heated by convection, which means transferring heat by vertical movements to higher levels and back to the ground.
options.c: Lower layers of air are heated by radiation, which means emitting short-wave radiation absorbed by air layers close to the ground.
options.d: Lower layers of air are heated by latent heat, which means evaporating water and condensation close to the ground.
